title: Baseline network test failing on v1.6.2
body:
On Fedora 30 and Fedora 31, Podman v1.6.2 is failing during the Network test portion of the Baseline tests.  I'm not sure if this is anticipated or not.    I'm not sure if this had failed prior.  The script to reproduce the issue:

```
#!/bin/bash
# test_podman_network.sh
# A script to be run at the command line with Podman installed.
# This should be run against a new kit to provide base level testing
# on a freshly installed machine with no images or container in
# play.  This currently needs to be run as root.
#
# To run this command:
#
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -d # Install and then deinstall Docker
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -n # Do not perform docker test
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -e # Stop on error
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh    # Continue on error
#

#######
# See if we want to stop on errors and/or install and then remove Docker.
#######
HOST_PORT="${HOST_PORT:-8080}"
showerror=0
installdocker=0
usedocker=1
while getopts "den" opt; do
    case "$opt" in
    d) installdocker=1
       ;;
    e) showerror=1
       ;;
    n) usedocker=0
       ;;
    esac
done

if [ "$installdocker" -eq 1 ] && [ "usedocker" -ne 0 ]
then
    echo "Script will install and then deinstall Docker."
fi

if [ "$showerror" -eq 1 ]
then
    echo "Script will stop on unexpected errors."
    set -e
fi

pkg_manager=`command -v dnf`
if [ -z "$pkg_manager" ]; then
    pkg_manager=`command -v yum`
fi

echo "Package manager binary: $pkg_manager"

########
# Test networking, bind mounting a file, stdin/stdout redirect
########
echo "Testing networking: ..."
port_test_failed=0
txt1="Hello, Podman"
echo "$txt1" > /tmp/hello.txt
podman run -d --name myweb -p "$HOST_PORT:80" -w /var/www -v /tmp/hello.txt:/var/www/index.txt busybox httpd -f -p 80
echo "$txt1" | podman exec -i myweb sh -c "cat > /var/www/index2.txt"
txt2=$( podman exec myweb cat /var/www/index2.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS1" || { echo "FAIL1"; port_test_failed=1; }
txt2=$( podman run --rm --net host busybox wget -qO - http://localhost:$HOST_PORT/index.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS2" || { echo "FAIL2"; port_test_failed=1; }
txt2=$( podman run --rm --net host busybox wget -qO - http://localhost:$HOST_PORT/index2.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS3" || { echo "FAIL3"; port_test_failed=1; }
# podman run --rm --net container:myweb --add-host myweb:127.0.0.1 busybox wget -qO - http://myweb/index.txt
rm /tmp/hello.txt
podman stop myweb
podman rm myweb
[ "0$port_test_failed" -eq 1 ] && [ "0$showerror" -eq 1 ] && {
  echo "networking test failed";
  exit -1;
}
```

Test 1 and Test 3 fail in the above:

```
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_network.sh
#!/bin/bash
# test_podman_network.sh
# A script to be run at the command line with Podman installed.
# This should be run against a new kit to provide base level testing
# on a freshly installed machine with no images or container in
# play.  This currently needs to be run as root.
#
# To run this command:
#
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -d # Install and then deinstall Docker
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -n # Do not perform docker test
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh -e # Stop on error
# /bin/bash -v test_podman_baseline.sh    # Continue on error
#

#######
# See if we want to stop on errors and/or install and then remove Docker.
#######
HOST_PORT="${HOST_PORT:-8080}"
showerror=0
installdocker=0
usedocker=1
while getopts "den" opt; do
    case "$opt" in
    d) installdocker=1
       ;;
    e) showerror=1
       ;;
    n) usedocker=0
       ;;
    esac
done

if [ "$installdocker" -eq 1 ] && [ "usedocker" -ne 0 ]
then
    echo "Script will install and then deinstall Docker."
fi

if [ "$showerror" -eq 1 ]
then
    echo "Script will stop on unexpected errors."
    set -e
fi

pkg_manager=`command -v dnf`
if [ -z "$pkg_manager" ]; then
    pkg_manager=`command -v yum`
fi

echo "Package manager binary: $pkg_manager"
Package manager binary: /usr/bin/dnf

########
# Test networking, bind mounting a file, stdin/stdout redirect
########
echo "Testing networking: ..."
Testing networking: ...
port_test_failed=0
txt1="Hello, Podman"
echo "$txt1" > /tmp/hello.txt
podman run -d --name myweb -p "$HOST_PORT:80" -w /var/www -v /tmp/hello.txt:/var/www/index.txt busybox httpd -f -p 80
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 7c9d20b9b6cd done
Copying config 19485c79a9 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
5658a6985fd0da8aab22423253e38ff3aee3a2472dd5c3e0f874dcfcf2b6d634
echo "$txt1" | podman exec -i myweb sh -c "cat > /var/www/index2.txt"
Error: read unixpacket @->/var/run/libpod/socket/7951c50ead57ba765aa85879ba2a1e5e77a6072e30601192f17ddb772e103676/attach: read: connection reset by peer
txt2=$( podman exec myweb cat /var/www/index2.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS1" || { echo "FAIL1"; port_test_failed=1; }
FAIL1
txt2=$( podman run --rm --net host busybox wget -qO - http://localhost:$HOST_PORT/index.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS2" || { echo "FAIL2"; port_test_failed=1; }
PASS2
txt2=$( podman run --rm --net host busybox wget -qO - http://localhost:$HOST_PORT/index2.txt )
[ "x$txt1" == "x$txt2" ] && echo "PASS3" || { echo "FAIL3"; port_test_failed=1; }
FAIL3
# podman run --rm --net container:myweb --add-host myweb:127.0.0.1 busybox wget -qO - http://myweb/index.txt
rm /tmp/hello.txt
podman stop myweb
5658a6985fd0da8aab22423253e38ff3aee3a2472dd5c3e0f874dcfcf2b6d634
podman rm myweb
5658a6985fd0da8aab22423253e38ff3aee3a2472dd5c3e0f874dcfcf2b6d634
[ "0$port_test_failed" -eq 1 ] && [ "0$showerror" -eq 1 ] && {
  echo "networking test failed";
  exit -1;
}
```

comments.body: May I know what happens if you reboot your system between every two tests? I've just upgraded to v1.6.2 (Ubuntu) and a weird thing that I've noticed is that somehow after a container that exposes a port and mounts a named volume is stopped (and removed), the port cannot be reused until I reboot my system. I'm running in root-less mode, not sure whether the same thing happens under root mode.

Rootfull and rootless networking have almost nothing in common.  If you see a port as still being locked in rootless podman, then I would think that their must be a process holding the socket open.  Perhaps slirp4netns is still running or some proces sof the container?  Or maybe the pause container.  
@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe WDYT?
this is simply the regression in run -i behaviour.  @mheon was looking into it before his pto.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@baude @mheon Do we still have this issue?
